title: App Entry Point (pages/page.tsx)
content:
This file serves as the main entry point for the application's homepage. It orchestrates the rendering of various UI components that constitute the landing page for DocMed.

## Purpose

The primary purpose of this file is to assemble and display the various sections of the DocMed homepage, including the navigation bar, hero section, trust bar, features, 'how it works' explanation, statistics, call-to-action, and footer.

## Key Components Rendered:

*   **`Navbar`**: Provides site navigation and user authentication links.
*   **`Hero`**: Features the main headline, a compelling description, and primary call-to-action buttons, often accompanied by an animated globe.
*   **`TrustBar`**: Displays logos of companies that trust the product, serving as social proof.
*   **`Features`**: Details the core functionalities of DocMed.
*   **`HowItWorks`**: Explains the step-by-step process of using DocMed.
*   **`Stats`**: Presents key metrics and achievements related to the product.
*   **`CTA`**: A prominent call-to-action section encouraging users to sign up or get started.
*   **`Footer`**: Contains essential links, company information, and legal notices.

## Usage Example

This file is automatically rendered by Next.js when a user navigates to the root of the application. Developers would typically modify this file to adjust the order or content of the homepage sections. For instance, to reorder the 'Features' and 'HowItWorks' sections, you would swap their positions within the `<main>` tag:

```typescript
export default function Home() {
  return (
    <main>
      <Navbar />
      <Hero />
      <TrustBar />
      {/* How it works section now appears before Features */}
      <HowItWorks />
      <Features /> 
      <Stats />
      <CTA />
      <Footer />
    </main>
  );
}
```
